 Issue 2.0 – November 2025 
 
DATA ACCESS      
 
After registration, you will be able to download our data. To assist you, our HelpCenter is 
available, and more specifically its section about download. 
Information on operational issues on products and services can be found on our User 
Notification Service. If you have any questions, please contact us. 
 
I INTRODUCTION 
I.1. Summary 
This document is the user manual for the Copernicus Marine Service       Ocean Monitoring 
Indicator (OMI) OMI_CIRCULATION_BOUNDARY_BLKSEA_rim_current_index     . It provides an 
estimate of the intensity of the Black Sea R     im C     urrent                starting in 1993 and 
extended operationally on a yearly basis up to one year before the current year     . 
The      product contains the Black Sea R     im C     urrent intensity index (BSRCI) calculated from 
the BLKSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_007_004 reanalysis product. The RCI is a dimensionless quantity, 
expressing the intensity of the R     im C     urrent in a particular year in relation to climatological 
average. 
The product is available for the Black Sea area and calculated as a time series with yearly 
frequency. Three time series      are provided, corresponding to the BSRCI calculated for the 
northern R     im current, the      Southern R     im C     urrent and the combination of the two 
areas. More details about the calculation can be found in the corresponding Quality Information 
Document (QUID) document.

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Product User Manual for the Copernicus Marine Service Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) OMI_CIRCULATION_BOUNDARY_BLKSEA_rim_current_index, providing details on the Black Sea Rim Current Intensity Index calculated from reanalysis data.
